pageCaption.value: The Pioneers is a brand new series that tells the stories of people improving the planet.

metadescription.value: Discover Monocle's initiative The Pioneers, a series of podcast that tells the stories of people improving the planet. More on rolex.org.

text.value:
The Pioneers is a brand new series that tells the stories of people improving the planet. You’ll hear how Rolex supports these innovators with whom they share a passion for safeguarding the earth for future generations.

Gain precious insight into the fresh thinking that is disrupting received wisdom for the better; and learn how fearless action continues to be the crucial driver of change.

pageTitle.value: Saving Rwanda’s bird of fortune - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Rwanda’s turbulent history has meant wildlife conservation has not always been able to be a priority, but the situation has improved over recent years. Olivier Nsengimana has been part of this change with an emblematic bird as his flagship species.

WaPageTitle.value: Saving Rwanda’s bird of fortune - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Rwanda’s turbulent history has meant wildlife conservation has not always been able to be a priority, but the situation has improved over recent years. Olivier Nsengimana has been part of this change with an emblematic bird as his flagship species.

previewTitle.value: Saving Rwanda’s bird of fortune - Rolex Awards

description.value: Rwandan veterinarian Olivier Nsengimana tells us about his mission to save the endangered grey-crowned crane, an elegant bird that represents wealth and prosperity but has been poached for people to keep as a pet.

pushInfoText.value: Rwandan veterinarian Olivier Nsengimana tells us about his mission to save the endangered grey-crowned crane, an elegant bird that represents wealth and prosperity but has been poached for people to keep as a pet.

pageTitle.value: Stars of the sea - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: To help protect the charismatic whale shark, the world’s biggest fish, Brad Norman created an identification system based on its celestial markings.

WaPageTitle.value: Stars of the sea - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: To help protect the charismatic whale shark, the world’s biggest fish, Brad Norman created an identification system based on its celestial markings.

previewTitle.value: Stars of the sea - Rolex Awards

description.value: The whale shark is the largest fish in the world, recognisable by its celestial markings. Australian scientist Brad Norman has been tracking this elusive and endangered creature by using technology designed to study stars, as well as images collected through ecotourism. Norman is based in Perth, where he runs his company ECOCEAN.

pushInfoText.value: The whale shark is the largest fish in the world, recognisable by its celestial markings. Australian scientist Brad Norman has been tracking this elusive and endangered creature by using technology designed to study stars, as well as images collected through ecotourism. Norman is based in Perth, where he runs his company ECOCEAN.

pageTitle.value: The hand puppet that saves lives - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Appalled by the deaths of thousands of children from preventable diseases in her country, an Ethiopian schoolteacher invented an entertaining way to inform youngsters about hygiene.

WaPageTitle.value: The hand puppet that saves lives - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Appalled by the deaths of thousands of children from preventable diseases in her country, an Ethiopian schoolteacher invented an entertaining way to inform youngsters about hygiene.

previewTitle.value: The hand puppet that saves lives

description.value: Former teacher Bruktawit Tigabu is teaching children in Ethiopia about hygiene through a hit TV show she created, Tsehai Loves Learning. It features a six-year-old giraffe who has become a beloved character watched by millions across Africa.

pushInfoText.value: Former teacher Bruktawit Tigabu is teaching children in Ethiopia about hygiene through a hit TV show she created, Tsehai Loves Learning. It features a six-year-old giraffe who has become a beloved character watched by millions across Africa.

pageCaption.value: Exploring subterranean caves in South America’s table-top mountains where no human has ever set foot, Italian geologist Francesco Sauro is not only finding clues to evolution on Earth – he is helping to prepare for expeditions to other planets.

metadescription.value: Exploring subterranean caves in South America’s table-top mountains where no human has ever set foot, Italian geologist Francesco Sauro is not only finding clues to evolution on Earth – he is helping to prepare for expeditions to other planets.

description.value: We speak to speleologist Francesco Sauro about the physical and psychological demands of cave exploration. He tells us what it's like to be the first person to set foot in a cave and how being a subterranean expert has enabled him to work with astronauts.

pushInfoText.value: We speak to speleologist Francesco Sauro about the physical and psychological demands of cave exploration. He tells us what it's like to be the first person to set foot in a cave and how being a subterranean expert has enabled him to work with astronauts.

pageTitle.value: Mark Kendall - Medical alerts from a microwearable sticker - Rolex.org

pageCaption.value: From creating a pain-free vaccine patch, Australian scientist Mark Kendall is now developing microwearable devices that send early warnings when patients are experiencing events like heart attacks.

WaPageTitle.value: Mark Kendall - Medical alerts from a microwearable sticker - Rolex.org

metadescription.value: Scientist and 2012 Rolex Laureate Mark Kendall has produced microwearable sensors that provide critical medical information. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Medical alerts from a sticker on your hand

description.value: We meet rocket scientist-turned-immunology expert Mark Kendall to find out about his Nanopatch, which could revolutionise vaccinations and eradicate some diseases.

pushInfoText.value: We meet rocket scientist-turned-immunology expert Mark Kendall to find out about his Nanopatch, which could revolutionise vaccinations and eradicate some diseases.

pageCaption.value: Millions of people live in the shadow of active volcanoes but Scottish physicist Andrew McGonigle is using drones to develop ways to predict eruptions.

metadescription.value: Millions of people live in the shadow of active volcanoes but Scottish physicist Andrew McGonigle is using drones to develop ways to predict eruptions.

description.value: We speak to volcanologist Andrew McGonigle about the technology he’s developed to get inside volcanoes, where he finds his inspiration and how volcanology can take you to space.

pushInfoText.value: We speak to volcanologist Andrew McGonigle about the technology he’s developed to get inside volcanoes, where he finds his inspiration and how volcanology can take you to space.

pageTitle.value: Women doctors for telemedicine in Pakistan - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Dr Sara Saeed is pairing female doctors with digital technology for remote access to healthcare. Discover how her telemedicine model saves lives and empowers women.

WaPageTitle.value: Women doctors for telemedicine in Pakistan - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Dr Sara Saeed is pairing female doctors with digital technology for remote access to healthcare. Discover how her telemedicine model saves lives and empowers women.

previewTitle.value: Women doctors for telemedicine in Pakistan

description.value: Andrew Mueller speaks to Sara Saeed, founder of Sehat Kahani, about the “doctor brides” phenomenon and what she is doing to get female doctors in Pakistan practising while also making healthcare accessible.

pushInfoText.value: Andrew Mueller speaks to Sara Saeed, founder of Sehat Kahani, about the “doctor brides” phenomenon and what she is doing to get female doctors in Pakistan practising while also making healthcare accessible.

pageCaption.value: Most of the world’s 285 million visually impaired people live in low-income countries, often in areas where there is little access to diagnosis or treatment. British ophthalmologist Andrew Bastawrous is radically changing eye care in sub-Saharan Africa with a portable examination system based on smartphones.

metadescription.value: Most of the world’s 285 million visually impaired people live in low-income countries, often in areas where there is little access to diagnosis or treatment. British ophthalmologist Andrew Bastawrous is radically changing eye care in sub-Saharan Africa with a portable examination system based on smartphones.

description.value: We meet eye surgeon and founder of Peek Vision, Andrew Bastawrous who is a leading force in making eye healthcare available for everyone.

pushInfoText.value: We meet eye surgeon and founder of Peek Vision, Andrew Bastawrous who is a leading force in making eye healthcare available for everyone.

pageTitle.value: Reef rescue with the toughest corals - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Marine biologist Emma Camp is out to find the world’s toughest corals. Discover how she plans to rescue reefs in the face of climate change and human activity.

WaPageTitle.value: Reef rescue with the toughest corals - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Marine biologist Emma Camp is out to find the world’s toughest corals. Discover how she plans to rescue reefs in the face of climate change and human activity.

previewTitle.value: Reef rescue with the toughest corals

description.value: We meet marine biologist Emma Camp as she sets off in search of resilient corals that could save some of the world’s reefs.

pushInfoText.value: We meet marine biologist Emma Camp as she sets off in search of resilient corals that could save some of the world’s reefs.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - How Greenland’s ice impacts climate change

pageCaption.value: A strange new life is blossoming within the icy carapace of Greenland, daubing thousands of square kilometres of pristine white landscape in an eerie patina of greens, reds, browns, purple and black.

WaPageTitle.value: Darkening hues of Greenland - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: 2016 Rolex Young Laureate Joseph Cook is carrying out research on the function of microscopic plant life in the melting of the planet’s ice sheets.

description.value: Robert Bound meets glaciologist Joseph Cook to find out what life is like on an ice sheet and how he’s using AI to map these vast, icy landscapes as they disappear.

pushInfoText.value: Robert Bound meets glaciologist Joseph Cook to find out what life is like on an ice sheet and how he’s using AI to map these vast, icy landscapes as they disappear.

pageTitle.value: Protect a giant fish for the Amazon - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Brazilian ecologist João Campos-Silva is working to protect the largest scaled freshwater fish in the world. Discover how doing so is lifting up local communities.

WaPageTitle.value: Protect a giant fish for the Amazon - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Brazilian ecologist João Campos-Silva is working to protect the largest scaled freshwater fish in the world. Discover how doing so is lifting up local communities.

previewTitle.value: Protect a giant fish for the Amazon - Rolex Awards

description.value: The Brazilian ecologist tells us about how his plans to save the arapaima — the giant endangered Amazonian fish — is not just about preserving a species but also about helping indigenous communities to survive.

pushInfoText.value: The Brazilian ecologist tells us about how his plans to save the arapaima – the giant endangered Amazonian fish – is not just about preserving a species but also about helping indigenous communities to survive.

pageTitle.value: Rapid malaria testing with no blood sample - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Ugandan IT specialist Brian Gitta has developed a portable device that detects malaria without a blood sample. Discover more about this powerful new medical technology.

WaPageTitle.value: Rapid malaria testing with no blood sample - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Ugandan IT specialist Brian Gitta has developed a portable device that detects malaria without a blood sample. Discover more about this powerful new medical technology.

previewTitle.value: Rapid malaria testing with no blood sample

description.value: We meet a Ugandan IT specialist who has found a way to diagnose malaria quickly, without drawing blood. He tells Andrew Mueller about his research and how he hopes to convince those affected to embrace new technology.

pushInfoText.value: We meet a Ugandan IT specialist who has found a way to diagnose malaria quickly, without drawing blood. He tells Andrew Mueller about his research and how he hopes to convince those affected to embrace new technology.

pageTitle.value: Reduce wildlife-human conflict in India - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Conservationist Krithi Karanth is working to reduce wildlife-human conflict in India. Discover how she is increasing trust between conservationists and communities.

WaPageTitle.value: Reduce wildlife-human conflict in India - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Conservationist Krithi Karanth is working to reduce wildlife-human conflict in India. Discover how she is increasing trust between conservationists and communities.

previewTitle.value: Reduce wildlife-human conflict in India - Rolex Awards

description.value: In rural India, relationships between humans and mammals are increasingly fractured. Dr Krithi Karanth tells us how she intervenes by helping farmers get compensated for damaged land and by teaching children that the animals aren’t all bad.

pushInfoText.value: In rural India, relationships between humans and mammals are increasingly fractured. Dr Krithi Karanth tells us how she intervenes by helping farmers get compensated for damaged land and by teaching children that the animals aren’t all bad.

suptitle.value: Women doctors for telemedicine in Pakistan

pageTitle.value: Gregoire Courtine - Help paralysed people walk again - Rolex.org

pageCaption.value: For scientist Grégoire Courtine, a broken back need no longer be a barrier to motion. Discover how his neuroprosthetic "bridge" is helping the paralysed walk again.

WaPageTitle.value: Gregoire Courtine - Help paralysed people walk again - Rolex.org

metadescription.value: For scientist Grégoire Courtine, a broken back need no longer be a barrier to motion. Discover how his neuroprosthetic "bridge" is helping the paralysed walk again.

description.value: We meet the French doctor making major advances in the treatment of paraplegic patients. He tells Andrew Mueller how he used his background in maths and physics to devise a spinal bridge that’s helping people to get moving.

pushInfoText.value: We meet the French doctor making major advances in the treatment of paraplegic patients. He tells Andrew Mueller how he used his background in maths and physics to devise a spinal bridge that’s helping people to get moving.

pageTitle.value: Recycling unrecyclable plastic waste - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Miranda Wang is developing unique technologies to turn plastic waste into valuable chemicals. Discover how her company is pioneering answers to the plastics crisis.

WaPageTitle.value: Recycling unrecyclable plastic waste - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Miranda Wang is developing unique technologies to turn plastic waste into valuable chemicals. Discover how her company is pioneering answers to the plastics crisis.

previewTitle.value: Recycling unrecyclable plastic waste - Rolex Awards

description.value: Our new series begins with Californian tech entrepreneur Miranda Wang, who’s been thinking about the plastic crisis since high school. Now in her mid-twenties, she tells Andrew Mueller about her company BioCellection, which could turn a third of the world's plastic waste into wealth.

Now in her mid-twenties, she tells Andrew Mueller about her company BioCellection, which could turn a third of the world's plastic waste into wealth.
